metadata.title: Build Maya's Three-Beat Verification Log with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I want to push the verification log past a single ticker. I'll ask Claude to run the three identical claude-3-5-haiku calls across a basket of five names, parse each Sharpe as a float, and log every spread. Then I'll wire in the yfinance primary-source row per ticker. Help me: (1) turn the 10% flag into a per-ticker table sorted by spread; (2) separate convention drift from genuine model disagreement using the primary source; (3) decide which flagged rows actually warrant re-checking a memo.

props.segment: Build Maya's Three-Beat Verification Log with

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: Three models, three Sharpes, one 15% spread.
Build it. Verify it. Break it.

beats.role_note: the problem, context, stakes -- BEFORE the CLI opens
beats.narration_text: Three models return NVDA Sharpes of 1.38, 1.41, and 1.62. The 15% spread could flip a memo — but there is a disciplined way to classify whether that is cosmetic or substantive. The three-beat method replaces the disciplines that spreadsheets enforced automatically: specification, editorial read, verification log.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- title card: 1.38 | 1.41 | 1.62. Same ticker, same prompt. 15% spread. Which one is right?

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to write the verification pipeline: three identical API calls to claude-3-5-haiku, parse each Sharpe as a float, print the spread, flag if greater than 10%.

props.command: claude "Write a Python script using the Anthropic SDK that sends three identical prompts to claude-3-5-haiku asking for NVDA vs SPY Sharpe ratio (36-month daily, √252 annualization, 4.5% risk-free rate). Parse each response to extract the Sharpe ratio as a float. Print a verification log table showing all three values and the spread. Flag the row if spread > 10%."

beats.narration_text: The script sends the same prompt three times, extracts the Sharpe ratio from each response, computes the spread as max minus min, and flags the row if spread exceeds 10%.

props.code:
import anthropic, re
client=anthropic.Anthropic()
prompt="What is the Sharpe ratio for NVDA vs SPY over 36 months using daily returns, √252 annualization, 4.5% risk-free rate? Return only the NVDA Sharpe ratio as a number."
values=[]
for i in range(3):
    msg=client.messages.create(model="claude-haiku-4-5",max_tokens=128,
        messages=[{"role":"user","content":prompt}])
    text=msg.content[0].text
    m=re.search(r"[0-9]+\.[0-9]+",text)
    v=float(m.group()) if m else None
    values.append(v)
    print(f"Run {i+1}: {v}")
spread=max(v for v in values if v)-min(v for v in values if v)
flag="FLAG" if spread>0.1 else "OK"
print(f"Spread: {spread:.2f} [{flag}]")

beats.role_note: first output -- screen-recording of terminal run
beats.narration_text: Three API responses stream in. The Sharpe values print line by line. The spread computes — 0.24 — and the FLAG appears. The verification log has named a substantive disagreement: 24% spread on the same ticker from the same model.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording mp4 of the terminal: three API responses appearing, Sharpe values printed, SPREAD: 0.24 [FLAG] appearing on the final line.

beats.narration_text: Add a fourth primary-source row: compute the NVDA Sharpe from yfinance directly and compare it to the three API responses. Show whether the primary source closes the disagreement or reveals a systematic bias.

props.command: claude "Modify the script: add a fourth row to the verification log populated by a manual yfinance computation of NVDA Sharpe (36-month daily, √252, 4.5% risk-free). Print all four rows as a table: run, value, spread from primary source. Flag rows where model value deviates from primary by more than 10%."

beats.narration_text: The primary source shows a Sharpe of 1.52. Two of three model responses are flagged: one is 9% below primary, one is 7% above. The spread across models reflects convention drift — not factual disagreement.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- screen-recording: four-row verification log table; primary source row shows 1.52; model rows flagged with deviations; convention drift hypothesis visible.

beats.narration_text: The three-beat method replaces the disciplines that spreadsheets enforced automatically: named ranges become specification, formula trace becomes editorial read, scenario tab becomes verification log. The pipeline makes the convention explicit so you argue about the assumption, not the number.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- recap card: SPECIFICATION. EDITORIAL READ. VERIFICATION LOG. The three beats that replace the spreadsheet disciplines.

beats.narration_text: Next: try adding a fourth primary-source row from yfinance and see if the model spread reflects convention drift or factual error. Follow for more computational finance builds.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE -- next-steps card: Add a primary source row. Is the spread convention drift or factual error?

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.
